name: saas-utility
description: Structural bias for SaaS product pages and screens where feature discovery, task density, and progressive disclosure support repeated, goal-oriented use.
intent: Surface the core feature or task immediately, enable progressive disclosure of depth, and keep navigation and feedback persistent without overwhelming the primary workflow.

sectionOrder.reason: SaaS product screens must orient the user around the current feature or task scope before exposing controls.
Core feature surfaces should be reachable without scrolling through marketing content or secondary options.
Feature configuration should be close to the primary surface but accessible on demand rather than always visible.
Confirmation, error, and status feedback must be proximate to the action that triggered it.
Feature discovery and upgrade paths should be accessible without disrupting the active workflow.

density.reason: Repeated-use SaaS screens benefit from density that reduces navigation steps, but onboarding and destructive actions require calm.

ctaCadence.secondaryActionRule: Secondary actions may be co-visible when they support the same task region and are visually subordinate.
ctaCadence.reason: SaaS utility screens can expose more actions than marketing pages, but each task region still needs a single clear primary operation.

overlayUse.reason: SaaS overlays preserve task context and allow in-flow configuration without full-page navigation.

antiPatterns.name: marketing-hero-on-product-screen
antiPatterns.reason: Product screens should orient around the active task, not re-sell the product to an existing user.

antiPatterns.name: deeply-nested-primary-controls
antiPatterns.reason: Primary feature controls buried in sequential drill-downs increase task time for repeated operations.

antiPatterns.name: ungrouped-density
antiPatterns.reason: Dense controls without visual grouping make feature discovery slower and increase error likelihood.

antiPatterns.name: feedback-disconnected-from-action
antiPatterns.reason: Success and error states must appear adjacent to the triggering action, not only in a global notification center.
